title: Tahia Anan Dhira
text:
Exporters of cultural products





The above map compares Weighted outdegree centrality of exporting countries for unique cultural products, reproducible cultural products, and non-cultural products in 2000 and 2023. Darker shades indicate greater export centrality. The figure illustrates the evolution of global trade networks, highlighting the increasing prominence of China alongside the United States over time. While reproducible cultural products such as books, music CDs have seen new exporters, unique cultural product export still remains concentrated in a very few European countries and in US.

title: Tahia Anan Dhira
text:
Importers of pollution





Ranking trajectories of the top 10 importers of CO₂ embodied in international trade. Countries are classified as advanced (blue) or emerging (orange) according to the IMF. The figure shows that advanced economies consistently comprise nearly nine of the ten largest importers, indicating their dominant role in outsourcing carbon emissions through international trade.

title: Tahia Anan Dhira
text:
Importers and Pollution





This bump chart illustrates the changing rankings of the top 10 exporters of CO₂ embodied in international trade. Blue lines represent advanced economies and orange lines represent emerging market economies, following the IMF classification. Throughout the study period, emerging market economies consistently account for approximately eight of the ten largest exporters of embodied CO₂.

title: Tahia Anan Dhira
text:
Research is the way I have chosen to run the machine.
But the machine sometimes runs toward elsewhere.
I maintain a separate website dedicated entirely to my artistic practice and portfolio work.

title: Code
text:
This page contains selected code snippets from my research projects. Full reproducible workflows are available on GitHub repositories.
```Stata code sample import delimited “data/cleaned/panel_complete.csv”, clear

Summary statistics

unique dyad unique year

GDP transformations

gen sum_gdp_pc = log_gdp_pc_d + log_gdp_pc gen abs_gdp_pc = abs(log_gdp_pc - log_gdp_pc_d)

Landlocked indicator

gen landlock = (landlocked == 1 & landlocked_d == 1) label variable landlock “1 both countries are landlocked, 0 any of them or both have seaports”

Development classification

gen dev = (development == 1) + 2*(development_d == 1) label variable dev “1 developing-developing, 2 developed-developing, 3 developing-developed, 4 developed-developed”
ssc install reghdfe
ssc install ppmlhdfe, replace
local outcomes unique reproducible noncultural_trade
foreach y of local outcomes { ppmlhdfe `y’ sum_gdp_pc abs_gdp_pc rta contig comlang_off colony comcol log_dist landlock i.dev, /// a(year) vce(cluster dyad) }
foreach y of local outcomes { ppmlhdfe y' sum_gdp_pc abs_gdp_pc hysteresis_y’ /// rta contig comlang_off colony comcol log_dist landlock i.dev, /// a(year) vce(cluster dyad) }
foreach y of local outcomes { ppmlhdfe `y’ sum_gdp_pc abs_gdp_pc rta contig comlang_off colony comcol log_dist landlock i.dev, /// a(dyad year) vce(cluster dyad) }
egen exp_year = group(iso_o year) egen imp_year = group(iso_d year)
foreach y of local outcomes { ppmlhdfe y' sum_gdp_pc abs_gdp_pc hysteresis_y’ /// rta contig comlang_off colony comcol log_dist landlock i.dev, /// a(exp_year imp_year) vce(cluster dyad) }
local outcomes unique reproducible noncultural_trade
foreach y of local outcomes { ppmlhdfe y' sum_gdp_pc abs_gdp_pc hysteresis_y’ /// rta contig comlang_off colony comcol log_dist landlock i.dev , /// a(dyad exp_year imp_year) vce(cluster dyad)
}

title: About

title: Tahia Anan Dhira
text:
Bilateral Convergence across the World: Does International Migration Matter? (working paper, 2026)
- Constructed a panel of directed weighted bilateral migration networks spanning 23 years.
- Developed reproducible workflows for network construction, visualization, and manuscript figures.
- Contributed to the conceptual framework and manuscript preparation.

Gendered Cultural Hierarchies in Social Media Audience Discourse: Evidence from Bangladeshi Artists (working paper, 2026)
- Developed a Python pipeline using the YouTube Data API v3 to collect and process more than 8 million comments.
- Translated almost 2.3 million Bangla texts to English.
- Transliterated almost 1.6 million Romanized Bangla texts to Bangla and then translated to English.
- Constructed a cleaned corpus of over 7 million YouTube comments for computational text analysis.
- Implemented reproducible data processing and analysis workflows in Python and R.

Product Reproducibility and Trade Patterns: Evidence from Cultural Goods (submitted, 2026)
- Constructed global trade networks for unique and reproducible cultural products, and non-cultural products.
- Estimated augmented gravity models using STERGMs and PPML regressions.
